title: Jugular foramen
body_html:
The jugular foramen is an opening in the skull base formed anteriorly by the petrous portion of the temporal bone and posteriorly by the occipital bone

It transmits the following:
  • Cranial nerve IX (Glossopharyngeal)
  • Cranial nerve X (Vagus)
  • Cranial nerve XI (Accessory)
  • Internal jugular vein

The RCEM syllabus expects candidates to be aware of the above cranial nerves emerging from the jugular foramen.

Image sourced from Wikipedia
